title: सीता मङ्गलाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सीता मङ्गलाष्टकम् ॥

जनकनन्दिन्यै सीतायै राघवप्रियवल्लभायै।
पतिव्रतानां शिरोमण्यै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ १॥

मिथिलायां समुद्भूतायै भूमिपुत्र्यै महात्मने।
धनुर्यज्ञविवाहितायै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ २॥

अयोध्यायां प्रतिष्ठितायै राजलक्ष्म्यै शुभावहायै।
कौसल्यानन्ददायिन्यै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ ३॥

वनवासे पतिव्रतायै तापस्यै धर्मचारिण्यै।
पर्णकुटीनिवासिन्यै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ ४॥

अशोकवने धीरचित्तायै रावणतिरस्कारिण्यै।
शीलसम्पत्तिरक्षिण्यै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ ५॥

अग्निपरीक्षाशुद्धायै पावनायै यशस्विन्यै।
लोकमातृस्वरूपायै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ ६॥

लवकुशजनन्यै वाल्मीकिगुरुसेवितायै।
धरणीगर्भगामिन्यै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ ७॥

महालक्ष्मीस्वरूपायै सर्वसौभाग्यदायिन्यै।
जगन्मातृस्वरूपायै श्रीसीतायै मङ्गलम्॥ ८॥

सीतामङ्गलकं स्तोत्रमिदं यः पठति प्रभाते।
सर्वसौभाग्यसम्प्राप्तिं सीताकृपां च विन्दति॥

॥ इति श्री सीता मङ्गलाष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sita Mangalashtakam ||

Janaka Nandinyai Sitayai Raghavapriya Vallabhayai |
Pativratanam Shiromanyai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 1 ||

Mithilayam Samudbhutayai Bhumiputryai Mahatmane |
Dhanuryajnavivahitayai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 2 ||

Ayodhyayam Pratishthitayai Rajalakshmyai Shubhavahayai |
Kausalyanandadayinyai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 3 ||

Vanavase Pativratayai Tapasyai Dharmacharinyai |
Parnakutinivasinyai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 4 ||

Ashokavane Dhirachittayai Ravana Tiraskarinyai |
Shilasampattirakshinyai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 5 ||

Agnipariksha Shuddhayai Pavanayai Yashasvinyai |
Lokamatru Svarupayai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 6 ||

Lavakushajananyai Valmikigurusevitayai |
Dharanigarbhagaminyai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 7 ||

Mahalakshmi Svarupayai Sarvasaubhagyadayinyai |
Jaganmatru Svarupayai Shri Sitayai Mangalam || 8 ||

Sitamangalakam Stotram Idam Yah Pathati Prabhate |
Sarvasaubhagya Sampraptim Sitakripam Cha Vindati ||

|| Iti Shri Sita Mangalashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
जनकनन्दिनी, राघवप्रिया, पतिव्रताओं में शिरोमणि सीता को मंगल।

मिथिला में उत्पन्न, भूमिपुत्री, धनुर्यज्ञ में विवाहित सीता को मंगल।

अयोध्या की राजलक्ष्मी, वनवास में पतिव्रता, अशोकवन में धीरचित्त सीता को मंगल।

अग्निपरीक्षा में शुद्ध, लवकुश जननी, महालक्ष्मी स्वरूप सीता को मंगल।

प्रातः पढ़ने से सर्वसौभाग्य और सीता कृपा प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Eight auspicious verses glorifying Mata Sita's life journey
Invokes sarva-saubhagya (all good fortune) to devotees
Strengthens pativrata shakti and family harmony
Invokes Sita as Mahalakshmi and Jaganmata
Ideal morning recitation for Sita Navami and Vivah Panchami
description: Sita Mangalashtakam — सीता मङ्गलाष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Goddess Sita. This revered text originates from Ananda Ramayana. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to strengthen pativrata shakti and family harmony as well as grant sarva-saubhagya (all good fortune) to devotees. Additionally, it is known to invoke Sita as Mahalakshmi and Jaganmata. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Sita Navami, Vivah Panchami, and Ram Navami to invoke divine grace. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 3 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Sita Mangalashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sita Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sita Mangalashtakam (सीता मङ्गलाष्टकम् — Eight Auspicious-Verse Hymn to Sita) is a Sanskrit stotra of eight verses (ashtakam) dedicated to Mata Sita — the consort of Sri Rama, daughter of King Janaka of Mithila (hence Janaki and Maithili), and incarnation of Goddess Lakshmi. Each of the eight verses ends with the refrain Shri Sitayai Mangalam — May auspiciousness be unto Sri Sita. The verses celebrate Sita's eight glorious aspects: (1) Janaka-nandini — daughter of Janaka, beloved of Raghava. (2) Mithila-samudbhuta Bhumi-putri — born in Mithila as daughter-of-Earth. (3) Ayodhya-Pratishthita Raja-lakshmi — established in Ayodhya as royal-Lakshmi. (4) Vanavasini Pativrata Tapasvi — forest-dwelling devoted-wife and ascetic. (5) Lanka-bandhini-Sundari — captive in Lanka but ever-radiant. (6) Agni-pravesha Pavitra — purified through fire-ordeal. (7) Vatsalya-mata Lava-Kusha — mother of Lava-Kusha. (8) Bhumi-praveshini Devi — returning to Earth-mother. The text is preserved in classical tradition. (Sources: Sita stotra collections; Tulsidas tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Mata Sita and her cosmic significance in Hindu tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Mata Sita is the divine feminine principle and the supreme adi-shakti of Sanatana Dharma — the consort (eternal wife) of Sri Rama and incarnation of Goddess Lakshmi. Born from a furrow (sita literally means furrow) when King Janaka was ploughing the field for a yajna in Mithila, she is also called Bhumija (earth-born), Janaki (daughter of Janaka), Maithili (princess of Mithila), Vaidehi (princess of Videha), and Janak-nandini. She married Sri Rama after he lifted Pinaka (Shiva's bow) at her swayamvara, accompanied him through 14 years of forest exile, was abducted by Ravana to Lanka, was rescued after the great Rama-Ravana war, gave birth to Lava and Kusha in Valmiki's ashram, and finally returned to her mother Earth (Bhumi-pravesha). For Hindus she is the supreme example of pativrata-dharma (devoted-wife righteousness), patience in suffering, and divine grace. She is worshipped both independently and inseparably with Rama as Sita-Rama. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana; Adbhuta Ramayana; Sita Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Janaka-nandinyai Sitayai Raghava-priya-vallabhayai?
seoFaqs.answer: Janaka-nandinyai Sitayai Raghava-priya-vallabhayai, Pativrata-naam Shiromanyai Sri Sitayai Mangalam — the opening verse of Sita Mangalashtakam — translates as: May auspiciousness (Mangalam) be unto Sri Sita (Sri Sitayai), who is the Daughter (Nandinyai) of Janaka (Janaka-nandinyai), the beloved-most-loved (Priya-vallabhayai) of Raghava (Sri Rama, descendant of Raghu), and the Crest-jewel (Shiromanyai — head-jewel) of all pativrata women (devoted wives). The verse establishes Sita's three supreme identities — (a) royal birth as Janaka's daughter, (b) divine spousal status as Rama's most-beloved consort, (c) ethical-spiritual eminence as the foremost example of devoted-wife dharma in all Hindu tradition. The closing refrain Shri Sitayai Mangalam — repeated in each of the eight verses — is itself a powerful mangala-mantra invoked at weddings, suhagan ceremonies, and women's vratas. (Sources: Sita Mangalashtakam verse 1; Sita-Rama tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting Sita Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Sita Mangalashtakam recitation is held to confer multiple auspicious benefits especially for women and households: (1) Sumangali yoga — for unmarried women, blessing of getting a virtuous Rama-like husband and timely marriage. (2) Marital harmony — for married women, deepening of love, mutual respect, and Sita-Rama-like partnership. (3) Saubhagya / akhanda saubhagya — long-lasting marital bliss, removal of widowhood-fear (vaidhavya-bhaya), protection of husband's longevity. (4) Putra-praapti — fertility and birth of virtuous children since Sita is the mother of Lava-Kusha. (5) Patience in suffering — Sita's endurance during Lanka-captivity and Valmiki-ashram exile teaches devotees patience. (6) Lakshmi-grace — since Sita is Lakshmi-incarnation, recitation brings prosperity and abundance. (7) Removal of family disputes and house-hold negativity. (8) Divine feminine empowerment — invoking Sita's strength to face life's trials. The hymn is especially recited during Vivah Panchami, Janaki Navami, Diwali, and women's vratas. (Sources: Sita upasana paddhati; suhagan vrata tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Sita Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Sita Mangalashtakam is most powerfully recited on the great Sita-Rama festival days: (1) Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla 5) — the supreme Sita-Rama wedding anniversary celebrated grandly at Janakpur (Nepal) and Ayodhya. (2) Janaki Navami / Sita Navami / Sita Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla 9) — Sita's birthday, the day she was discovered in Janaka's furrow. (3) Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla 9) — Sri Rama's birthday, equally honoring Sita as ardha-angini. (4) Akshay Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla 3) — for sumangali blessings. (5) Karva Chauth (Kartik Krishna 4) — North Indian women's husband-longevity vrata where Sita is invoked. (6) Vat Savitri (Jyeshtha Purnima) — pativrata vrata. (7) Every Tuesday (Hanuman day, since Hanuman is Sita's son). (8) Navaratri ashtami-navami days. Daily recitation is also recommended for unmarried girls seeking marriage and married women seeking marital harmony. The hymn is recited facing east, before a Sita-Rama-Lakshman-Hanuman picture, with red flowers, sindoor, mangalsutra-style offerings, and a kalash of water. (Sources: Janakpur Vivah Mandap paddhati; Ayodhya Sita-Rama tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Sita Mangalashtakam different from other Sita stotras and Lakshmi stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: All are Devi devotional texts but differ in scope and focus. (1) Sita Mangalashtakam — eight short Sanskrit verses celebrating eight aspects of Sita's life-leela, all ending with mangalam refrain; ideal for daily recitation in 3-5 minutes especially for women. (2) Sita Sahasranama (from Adbhuta Ramayana) — 1000 names of Sita, the most exhaustive Sita-naming text. (3) Sita Ashtottara Shatanama Stotra — 108 names of Sita, popular medium-length Sita stotra. (4) Sita-Rama Stuti and Janaki Mangal (by Tulsidas) — Hindi devotional poetry. (5) Sri Suktam (Rigvedic Khila) — invokes Lakshmi for prosperity. (6) Lakshmi Ashtottara, Lakshmi Sahasranama — for general Lakshmi-prosperity. While Lakshmi stotras emphasize abundance and material prosperity, Sita stotras (including this Mangalashtakam) emphasize pativrata-dharma, marital virtues, patience-in-suffering, and the ideal of Hindu womanhood. The Sita Mangalashtakam is uniquely valued for its concise eight-fold celebration of Sita's complete life-journey. (Sources: Sita upasana literature; comparative Devi stotra tradition.)
